message:
fix(og-image): Use streaming reader pattern to prevent memory exhaustion

Replaces the single arrayBuffer() call with a streaming reader pattern
that enforces size limits incrementally during download:

Before:
- Fetched entire image into memory with response.arrayBuffer()
- Only checked size AFTER full download completed
- Memory spike for large images before rejection

After:
- Uses response.body.getReader() for chunked streaming
- Tracks receivedLength incrementally as chunks arrive
- Cancels reader immediately when MAX_IMAGE_SIZE exceeded
- Combines chunks into final Uint8Array only after validation
- Properly handles stream read errors with try/catch

This prevents memory exhaustion attacks where malicious actors could
send oversized images to consume server memory before the size check
kicked in. The streaming approach rejects oversized content early in
the download process.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
